    <meta content="This guideline covers recognising and responding to abuse and neglect in children and young people aged under 18. It covers physical, sexual and emotional abuse, and neglect. The guideline aims to help anyone whose work brings them into contact with children and young people to spot signs of abuse and neglect and to know how to respond. It also supports practitioners who carry out assessments and provide early help and interventions to children, young people, parents and carers" name="Description" />

<div class="chapter" title="Recommendations" id="ng76-recommendations" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">
  <h2 class="title" id="recommendations">Recommendations</h2>
  <p>Recommendations in sections 1.1 to 1.3 are for anyone whose work brings them into contact with children, young people, parents and carers including those who work in early years, social care, health (including staff in A&amp;E and health drop‑in settings), education (including schools), the police, the voluntary and community sector, youth justice services and adult services. </p>
  <div class="section" title="1.1 Principles for working with children, young people, parents and carers" id="ng76-principles-for-working-with-children-young-people-parents-and-carers">
    <h3 class="title" id="principles-for-working-with-children-young-people-parents-and-carers">1.1 Principles for working with children, young people, parents and carers</h3>
    <div class="section" title="Working with children and young people" id="ng76-working-with-children-and-young-people">
      <h4 class="title" id="working-with-children-and-young-people">Working with children and young people</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.1</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Take a child-centred approach to all work with <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#children-and-young-people">children and young people</a>. Involve them in decision-making to the fullest extent possible depending on their age and developmental stage.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.2</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Use a range of methods (for example, drawing, books or activities if appropriate) for communicating with children and young people. Tailor communication to:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>their age and developmental stage</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>any disabilities, for example learning disabilities, neurodevelopmental disorders and hearing and visual impairments, seeking assistance from specialists if needed</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>communication needs, for example by using communication aids or providing an interpreter (ensure the interpreter is not a family member).</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.3</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>In all conversations with children and young people where there are concerns about <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#child-abuse-and-neglect" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#child-abuse-and-neglect">child abuse and neglect</a>:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>explain confidentiality and when you might need to share specific information, and with whom</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>be sensitive and empathetic</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>listen actively and use open questions </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>find out their views and wishes </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>check your understanding of what the child has told you</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>be sensitive to any religious or cultural beliefs</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>use plain language and explain any technical terms</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>work at the child or young person's pace</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>give them opportunities to stop the conversation or leave the room, and follow up if this does happen</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>explain what will happen next and when.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.4</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Make sure that conversations take place somewhere private and where the child or young person feels comfortable. Take account of any sensory issues the child or young person may have.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.5</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>If your interaction with a child or young person involves touching them (for example, a medical examination) explain what you are going to do and ask for consent: </p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>from them if they are over 16 (follow the <a class="link" href="http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents" target="_top">Mental Capacity Act 2005</a>) or under 16 but <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/reference-guide-to-consent-for-examination-or-treatment-second-edition" target="_top">Gillick competent</a> <strong>or</strong></p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>from their <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#parent-or-carer" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#parent-or-carer">parent or carer </a>if they are under 16 and not Gillick competent.<br /><br />If the child, young person or parent does not agree, respect their wishes unless touching them is essential to their treatment (seek legal advice first unless the need for treatment is immediate). <br /><br />For more guidance on seeking consent for medical examination in children and young people see the <a class="link" href="http://www.gmc-uk.org/guidance/ethical_guidance/children_guidance_index.asp" target="_top">General Medical Council's 0-18 years: guidance for all doctors</a>.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_6" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.6</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Produce a record of conversations with children and young people about child abuse and neglect, and any subsequent interventions as appropriate to their age, developmental stage and language abilities. This could be in writing or another format suitable to meet the child or young person's communication needs. Ensure that you:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Accurately represent their words, using their actual words unless there is a good reason not to, for example if this would include information about another child or young person.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Check that they have understood and agree with what is recorded (this could include both of you signing a written record) and record any disagreements. </p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_7" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.7</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Share reports and plans with the child or young person in a way that is appropriate to their age and understanding.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_8" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.8</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Clearly explain how you will work together with children and young people and do what you have said you will do. If circumstances change and this is no longer possible, explain why as soon as possible, and offer alternative actions.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_9" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.9</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Agree with the child or young person (if age appropriate) how you will communicate with each other. Give them contact details, including for services available out of hours. When contacting them: </p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>be aware of safety issues such as whether a perpetrator of abuse may have access to a young person's phone</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>agree what will happen if you contact them and they do not respond, for example following up with their nominated emergency contact.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Working with parents and carers" id="ng76-working-with-parents-and-carers">
      <h4 class="title" id="working-with-parents-and-carers">Working with parents and carers</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_10" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.10</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Aim to build good working relationships with parents and carers to encourage their engagement and continued participation. This should involve:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>actively listening to them, and helping them to deal with any emotional impact of your involvement with their family</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>being open and honest</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>seeking to empower them and engaging them in finding solutions</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>avoiding blame, even if they may be responsible for the child abuse or neglect</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>inviting, recognising and discussing any worries they have about specific interventions they will be offered</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>identifying what they are currently doing well, and building on this</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>making adjustments for any factors which may make it harder for them to get support, such as refugee status, long-term illness, neurodevelopmental disorders, mental health problems, disability or learning disability</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>being sensitive to religious or cultural beliefs</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>working in a way that enables trust to develop while maintaining professional boundaries</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>maintaining professional curiosity and questioning while building good relationships.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_11" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.11</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>When working with parents and carers:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>be reliable and available as promised</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>provide clear information about who to contact, including in an emergency </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>keep them informed, including explaining what information has been shared, and with whom</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>support people's communication needs, for example by using communication aids or providing an interpreter</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>agree records of any conversations, and share relevant documents and plans</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>be clear about the issues and concerns that have led to your involvement, and inform parents and carers if those concerns are resolved</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>be clear about the legal context in which your involvement with them is taking place.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Working with other practitioners" id="ng76-working-with-other-practitioners">
      <h4 class="title" id="working-with-other-practitioners">Working with other practitioners</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_12" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.12</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Coordinate your work with <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#practitioner" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#practitioner">practitioners</a> in other agencies so that children, young people, parents and carers do not need to give the same information repeatedly, in line with the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/safeguarding-practitioners-information-sharing-advice" target="_top">Department for Education's advice on information sharing: advice for practitioners providing safeguarding services</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Critical thinking and analysis" id="ng76-critical-thinking-and-analysis">
      <h4 class="title" id="critical-thinking-and-analysis">Critical thinking and analysis</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_1_13" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.1.13</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Present information critically and analytically and do not rely solely on protocols, proformas and electronic recording systems to support your professional thinking and planning.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.2 Factors that increase vulnerability to child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-factors-that-increase-vulnerability-to-child-abuse-and-neglect">
    <h3 class="title" id="factors-that-increase-vulnerability-to-child-abuse-and-neglect">1.2 Factors that increase vulnerability to child abuse and neglect</h3>
    <p>Vulnerability factors are factors that are known to increase the risk of child abuse and neglect. The presence of these factors does not mean that child abuse or neglect will occur, but practitioners should use their professional judgement to assess their significance in a particular child, young person or family. They should be considered in conjunction with the alerting features in <a class="link" href="recommendations#recognising-child-abuse-and-neglect" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#recognising-child-abuse-and-neglect">section 1.3</a>.</p>
    <p>These recommendations add further detail to the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/what-to-do-if-youre-worried-a-child-is-being-abused--2" target="_top">Department for Education's what to do if you're worried a child is being abused</a> about factors that increase vulnerability to child abuse and neglect.</p>
    <article id="ng76-1_2_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.2.1</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Recognise that <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#vulnerability-factor" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#vulnerability-factor">vulnerability factors</a> can be interrelated, and that separate factors can combine to increase the risk of harm to a child or young person. </p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <article id="ng76-1_2_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.2.2</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Take into account socioeconomic vulnerability factors for child abuse and neglect, such as poverty and poor housing.</p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <div class="section" title="Family factors" id="ng76-family-factors">
      <h4 class="title" id="family-factors">Family factors</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_2_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.2.3</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that the following parental factors increase vulnerability to child abuse and neglect, and that these may be compounded if the parent or carer lacks support from family or friends:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Substance misuse problems.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>A history of <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#domestic-abuse" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#domestic-abuse">domestic abuse</a>, including sexual violence or exploitation.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Emotional volatility or having problems managing anger.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Mental health problems which have a significant impact on the tasks of parenting.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_2_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.2.4</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise the following as vulnerability factors for recurring or persistent child abuse and neglect:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>The parent or carer does not engage with services.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>There have been 1 or more previous episodes of child abuse or neglect.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>The parent or carer has a mental health or substance misuse problem which has a significant impact on the tasks of parenting.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>There is chronic parental stress.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>The parent or carer experienced abuse or neglect as a child.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_2_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.2.5</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#neglect" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#neglect">neglect</a> and <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#emotional-abuse" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#emotional-abuse">emotional abuse</a> may be more likely to recur or persist than other forms of abuse.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Child factors" id="ng76-child-factors">
      <h4 class="title" id="child-factors">Child factors</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_2_6" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.2.6</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Be aware of the impact of a child or young person's age or gender on their vulnerability to child abuse and neglect, and the likelihood of recognition. For example, boys and young men may be less likely to disclose sexual exploitation (see also the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/child-sexual-exploitation-definition-and-guide-for-practitioners" target="_top">Department for Education's guidance on child sexual exploitation</a>).</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_2_7" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.2.7</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#disabled-children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#disabled-children-and-young-people">disabled children and young people</a> are more vulnerable to child abuse or neglect.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.3 Recognising child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-recognising-child-abuse-and-neglect">
    <h3 class="title" id="recognising-child-abuse-and-neglect">1.3 Recognising child abuse and neglect</h3>
    <p>These recommendations add further detail to the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/what-to-do-if-youre-worried-a-child-is-being-abused--2" target="_top">Department for Education's what to do if you're worried a child is being abused</a> on <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#alerting-feature" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#alerting-feature">alerting features</a> for child abuse and neglect. </p>
    <div class="section" title="Children and young people telling others about child abuse or neglect" id="ng76-children-and-young-people-telling-others-about-child-abuse-or-neglect">
      <h4 class="title" id="children-and-young-people-telling-others-about-child-abuse-or-neglect">Children and young people telling others about child abuse or neglect</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.1</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that children and young people who are being abused or neglected may find it difficult to tell someone for the first time because:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>they may have feelings of confusion, shame, guilt and of being stigmatised</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>they may not recognise their own experiences as abusive or neglectful</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>they may be being coerced by (or may be attached to) the person or people abusing or neglecting them</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>they may fear the consequences of telling someone, for example that no one will believe them, the abuse or neglect might get worse, their family will be split up or excluded by their community, or they will go into care</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>they may have communication difficulties or may not speak English fluently.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.2</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that children and young people who are being abused or neglected may not acknowledge this when asked, or may not want others to know.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.3</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that children and young people may communicate their abuse or neglect indirectly through their behaviour and appearance (see recommendations 1.3.12 to 1.3.47 and the <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg89" target="_top" data-original-url="http://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg89">NICE guideline on child maltreatment</a>).</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.4</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Take into account that when children and young people communicate their abuse or neglect (either directly or indirectly), it may refer to non-recent abuse or neglect.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.5</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Explore your concerns with children and young people in a non-leading way, for example by using open questions, if you are worried that they may be being abused or neglected. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_6" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.6</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Avoid causing possible prejudice to any formal investigation during early conversations about child abuse and neglect with children and young people by following guidance in the <a class="link" href="https://www.cps.gov.uk/legal-guidance/achieving-best-evidence-criminal-proceedings-guidance-interviewing-victims-and" target="_top">Ministry of Justice's achieving best evidence in criminal proceedings</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_7" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.7</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>If a child or young person tells you they have been abused or neglected, make a referral to children's social care using your local procedures. Explain to the child or young person who you will need to tell, and discuss what will happen next and when. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_8" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.8</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For people working in <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#regulated-profession" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#regulated-profession">regulated professions</a> (healthcare professionals and teachers), if a girl or young woman tells you they have experienced <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#female-genital-mutilation" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#female-genital-mutilation">female genital mutilation</a> (FGM), or you observe physical signs of FGM, you must report this to the police, in line with <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/mandatory-reporting-of-female-genital-mutilation-procedural-information" target="_top">Home Office guidance on mandatory reporting of female genital mutilation</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Supporting staff to recognise child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-supporting-staff-to-recognise-child-abuse-and-neglect">
      <h4 class="title" id="supporting-staff-to-recognise-child-abuse-and-neglect">Supporting staff to recognise child abuse and neglect</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_9" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.9</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Senior managers should ensure staff working in community settings, including education, can recognise and respond to child abuse and neglect and are aware of child safeguarding guidance relevant to their profession, for example the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/keeping-children-safe-in-education--2" target="_top">Department for Education's keeping children safe in education</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_10" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.10</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Commissioners should ensure all practitioners working in primary care can recognise and respond to child abuse and neglect. Ways to achieve this include:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>ensuring that newly qualified practitioners receive training in line with an approved training programme, for example levels 1 to 3 in the <a class="link" href="https://www.rcn.org.uk/professional-development/publications/pub-007366" target="_top">Royal College of Paediatrics and Child Health's safeguarding children and young people: roles and competencies for healthcare staff</a>. This should include an understanding of vulnerability factors for child abuse and neglect, such as parental mental health problems, alcohol and substance misuse </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>giving information to newly qualified practitioners, for example about local resources such as children's centres and parenting groups</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>giving practitioners advice on how to make a referral to social care.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_11" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.11</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For guidance on training health and social care practitioners to respond to domestic violence and abuse, follow recommendations 15 and 16 in the <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph50" target="_top" data-original-url="http://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph50">NICE guideline on domestic violence and abuse</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Alerting features for child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-alerting-features-for-child-abuse-and-neglect">
      <h4 class="title" id="alerting-features-for-child-abuse-and-neglect">Alerting features for child abuse and neglect</h4>
      <p>This section describes indicators that should alert practitioners to the possibility of child abuse or neglect. It may be child abuse or neglect occurring now or that has occurred in the past. Note that physical injuries and other clinical indicators are covered in the <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg89" target="_top" data-original-url="http://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg89">NICE guideline on child maltreatment</a>.</p>
      <p>In this section we use 'consider' and 'suspect' (as defined in the NICE guideline on child maltreatment) to indicate the extent to which an alerting feature suggests child abuse and neglect, with 'suspect' indicating stronger evidence of child abuse and neglect.</p>
      <div class="section" title="How to use these recommendations" id="ng76-how-to-use-these-recommendations">
        <h5 class="title" id="how-to-use-these-recommendations">How to use these recommendations</h5>
        <p>If a child is in immediate danger, refer to children's social care and/or the police. Otherwise, in response to any of the alerting features in this section, please follow the steps below in line with the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/working-together-to-safeguard-children--2" target="_top">Department for Education's statutory guidance on working together to safeguard children</a>. </p>
        <p>
          <strong>For all alerting features:</strong>
        </p>
        <div class="itemizedlist">
          <ul class="itemizedlist">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Seek advice from named or designated colleagues or your organisation's safeguarding lead. </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Speak to the child or young person as detailed in recommendations 1.3.5 and 1.3.6, if this is age and developmentally appropriate and it is safe to do so.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <p>
          <strong>For recommendations starting with 'suspect':</strong>
        </p>
        <div class="itemizedlist">
          <ul class="itemizedlist">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Discuss the need for a referral with children's social care using local multi-agency safeguarding procedures.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <p>
          <strong>For recommendations starting with 'consider':</strong>
        </p>
        <div class="itemizedlist">
          <ul class="itemizedlist">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Look for other alerting features in the child or young person's history, presentation or interactions with their parents or carers, now or in the past. </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Gather information from other agencies and explain to the family that this information is needed to make an overall assessment of the child or young person. If this is likely to place the child or young person at risk, seek advice from children's social care.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Make sure a review of the child or young person takes place, with the timing depending on your level of concern. Continue to look out for the alerting feature being repeated, or for any other alerting features.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
        <p>After taking these steps, if your level of concern increases to 'suspect', discuss the need for a referral with children's social care. If you conclude that a referral to children's social care is not required, you may wish to undertake or make a referral for <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#early-help" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#early-help">early help</a> assessment in line with local procedures.</p>
        <p>As highlighted in the recommendations below, alerting features for child abuse and neglect can be similar to behaviours arising from other causes, such as other stressful life experiences or neurodevelopmental disorders such as autism. However, practitioners should continue to consider the possibility of child abuse or neglect as a cause for behavioural and emotional alerting features, even if they are seemingly explained by another cause. </p>
        <p>Practitioners should also recognise that alerting features may be due to non-recent child abuse or neglect. If the alerting features relate to past child abuse or neglect, but the child or young person is now in a place of safety (for example, in an adoptive family), assess the child or young person to see what support they and their parent, carer, foster carer or adoptive parent need to cope with the consequences of the child abuse or neglect.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Behavioural and emotional alerting features" id="ng76-behavioural-and-emotional-alerting-features">
      <h4 class="title" id="behavioural-and-emotional-alerting-features">Behavioural and emotional alerting features</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_12" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.12</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child or young person displays or is reported to display a marked change in behaviour or emotional state (see examples below) that is a departure from what would be expected for their age and developmental stage and is not fully explained by a known stressful situation that is not part of child abuse and neglect (for example, bereavement or parental separation) or medical cause. Examples include:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>recurrent nightmares containing similar themes</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>extreme distress</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>markedly oppositional behaviour</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>withdrawal of communication</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>becoming withdrawn.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_13" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.13</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child's behaviour or emotional state is not consistent with their age and developmental stage or cannot be fully explained by medical causes, neurodevelopmental disorders (for example, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder [ADHD], autism spectrum disorders) or other stressful situation that is not part of child abuse or neglect (for example, bereavement or parental separation). Examples of behaviour or emotional states that may fit this description include:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Emotional states:</p>
              <p>
                <div class="itemizedlist">
                  <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>fearful, withdrawn, low self-esteem</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul>
                </div>
              </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Behaviour:</p>
              <p>
                <div class="itemizedlist">
                  <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>aggressive, oppositional</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>habitual body rocking</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul>
                </div>
              </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Interpersonal behaviours:</p>
              <p>
                <div class="itemizedlist">
                  <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>indiscriminate contact or affection seeking</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>over-friendliness to strangers including healthcare professionals</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>excessive clinginess</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>persistently resorting to gaining attention</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>demonstrating excessively 'good' behaviour to prevent parental or carer disapproval</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>failing to seek or accept appropriate comfort or affection from an appropriate person when significantly distressed</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>coercive controlling behaviour towards parents or carers</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>lack of ability to understand and recognise emotions</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>very young children showing excessive comforting behaviours when witnessing parental or carer distress.</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul>
                </div>
              </p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_14" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.14</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child shows repeated, extreme or sustained emotional responses that are out of proportion to a situation and are not expected for the child's age or developmental stage or fully explained by a medical cause, neurodevelopmental disorder (for example, ADHD, autism spectrum disorders) or bipolar disorder and the effects of any known past abuse or neglect have been explored. Examples of these emotional responses include:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>anger or frustration expressed as a temper tantrum in a school-aged child</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>frequent rages at minor provocation</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>distress expressed as inconsolable crying.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_15" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.15</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child shows dissociation (transient episodes of detachment that are outside the child's control and that are distinguished from daydreaming, seizures or deliberate avoidance of interaction) that is not fully explained by a known traumatic event unrelated to maltreatment.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_16" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.16</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider current or <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#past-child-abuse-or-neglect" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#past-child-abuse-or-neglect">past child abuse or neglect</a> if children or young people are showing any of the following behaviours:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>substance or alcohol misuse </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>self-harm </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>eating disorders</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>suicidal behaviours </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>
                <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#bullying" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#bullying">bullying</a> or being bullied.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_17" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.17</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child or young person has run away from home or care, or is living in alternative accommodation without the full agreement of their parents or carers.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_18" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.18</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child or young person regularly has responsibilities that interfere with the child's essential normal daily activities (for example, school attendance).</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_19" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.19</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a child responds to a health examination or assessment in an unusual, unexpected or developmentally inappropriate way (for example, extreme passivity, resistance or refusal).</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <div class="section" title="Sexual behavioural alerting features" id="ng76-sexual-behavioural-alerting-features">
        <h5 class="title" id="sexual-behavioural-alerting-features">Sexual behavioural alerting features</h5>
        <p>For more guidance about responding to potentially harmful sexual behaviours, see the <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng55" target="_top" data-original-url="http://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng55">NICE guideline on harmful sexual behaviour among children and young people</a>.</p>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_20" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.20</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect current or past child abuse and neglect if a child or young person's sexual behaviour is indiscriminate, precocious or coercive.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_21" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.21</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect child abuse and neglect, and in particular <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#sexual-abuse" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#sexual-abuse">sexual abuse</a>, if a pre‑pubertal child displays or is reported to display repeated or coercive sexualised behaviours or preoccupation (for example, sexual talk associated with knowledge, emulating sexual activity with another child).</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_22" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.22</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect sexual abuse if a pre-pubertal child displays or is reported to display unusual sexualised behaviours. Examples include:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>oral–genital contact with another child or a doll</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>requesting to be touched in the genital area</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>inserting or attempting to insert an object, finger or penis into another child's vagina or anus.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Alerting features for child physical neglect" id="ng76-alerting-features-for-child-physical-neglect">
        <h5 class="title" id="alerting-features-for-child-physical-neglect">Alerting features for child physical neglect</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_23" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.23</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect child abuse and neglect if a child repeatedly scavenges, steals, hoards or hides food with no medical explanation (for example <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#prader-willi-syndrome" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#prader-willi-syndrome">Prader–Willi syndrome</a>).</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_24" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.24</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect neglect if you repeatedly observe or hear reports of any of the following in the home that is in the parents' or carers' control: </p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>a poor standard of hygiene that affects a child's health</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>inadequate provision of food </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>a living environment that is unsafe for the child's developmental stage. <br /><br />Be aware that it may be difficult to distinguish between neglect and material poverty. However, care should be taken to balance recognition of the constraints on the parents' or carers' ability to meet their children's needs for food, clothing and shelter with an appreciation of how people in similar circumstances have been able to meet those needs.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_25" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.25</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect neglect if a child is persistently smelly and dirty. Take into account that children often become dirty and smelly during the course of the day. Use judgement to determine if persistent lack of provision or care is a possibility. Examples include:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>child seen at times of the day when it is unlikely that they would have had an opportunity to become dirty or smelly (for example, an early morning visit) </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>if the dirtiness is ingrained.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_26" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.26</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if a child has severe and persistent infestations, such as scabies or head lice.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_27" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.27</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if a child's clothing or footwear is consistently inappropriate (for example, for the weather or the child's size). Take into account that instances of inadequate clothing that have a suitable explanation (for example, a sudden change in the weather, slippers worn because they were closest to hand when leaving the house in a rush) or resulting from behaviour associated with neurodevelopmental disorders such as autism would not be alerting features for possible neglect.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Alerting features relating to child development" id="ng76-alerting-features-relating-to-child-development">
        <h5 class="title" id="alerting-features-relating-to-child-development">Alerting features relating to child development</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_28" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.28</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if a child displays <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#faltering-growth" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#faltering-growth">faltering growth</a> because of lack of provision of an adequate or appropriate diet. NICE has produced a <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng75" target="_top" data-original-url="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng75">guideline on faltering growth</a>.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_29" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.29</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider current or past physical or emotional child abuse or neglect if a child under 12 shows poorer than expected language abilities for their overall development (particularly in their ability to express their thoughts, wants and needs) that is not explained by other factors, for example neurodevelopmental difficulties or speaking English as a second language.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Alerting features relating to interactions between children and young people and parents or carers" id="ng76-alerting-features-relating-to-interactions-between-children-and-young-people-and-parents-or-carers">
        <h5 class="title" id="alerting-features-relating-to-interactions-between-children-and-young-people-and-parents-or-carers">Alerting features relating to interactions between children and young people and parents or carers</h5>
        <p>These recommendations assume that practitioners are seeing a parent or carer and child interacting.</p>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_30" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.30</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect or <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#physical-abuse" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#physical-abuse">physical abuse</a> if a child's behaviour towards their parent or carer shows any of the following, particularly if they are not observed in the child's other interactions:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>dislike or lack of cooperation </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>lack of interest or low responsiveness</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>high levels of anger or annoyance</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>seeming passive or withdrawn.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_31" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.31</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider emotional abuse if there is concern that parent– or carer–child interactions may be harmful. Examples include:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>Negativity or hostility towards a child or young person.</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>Rejection or scapegoating of a child or young person.</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>Developmentally inappropriate expectations of or interactions with a child, including inappropriate threats or methods of disciplining.</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>Exposure to frightening or traumatic experiences. </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>Using the child for the fulfilment of the adult's needs (for example, in marital disputes).</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>Failure to promote the child's appropriate socialisation (for example, involving children in unlawful activities, isolation, not providing stimulation or education).</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_32" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.32</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect emotional abuse if the interactions observed in recommendation 1.3.31 are persistent.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_33" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.33</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider emotional neglect if there is emotional unavailability and unresponsiveness from the parent or carer towards a child or young person and in particular towards an infant.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_34" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.34</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect emotional neglect if the interaction observed in recommendation 1.3.33 is persistent.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_35" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.35</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if parents or carers are seen or reported to punish a child for wetting or soiling despite practitioner advice that the symptom is involuntary.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_36" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.36</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider child abuse and neglect if a parent or carer refuses to allow a child or young person to speak to a practitioner on their own when it is necessary for the assessment of the child or young person.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Alerting features relating to supervision by parents or carers" id="ng76-alerting-features-relating-to-supervision-by-parents-or-carers">
        <h5 class="title" id="alerting-features-relating-to-supervision-by-parents-or-carers">Alerting features relating to supervision by parents or carers</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_37" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.37</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if parents or carers persistently fail to anticipate dangers and to take precautions to protect their child from harm. However, take into account that achieving a balance between an awareness of risk and allowing children freedom to learn by experience can be difficult.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_38" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.38</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if the explanation for an injury (for example, a burn, sunburn or an ingestion of a harmful substance) suggests a lack of appropriate supervision.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Alerting features relating to providing access to medical care or treatment" id="ng76-alerting-features-relating-to-providing-access-to-medical-care-or-treatment">
        <h5 class="title" id="alerting-features-relating-to-providing-access-to-medical-care-or-treatment">Alerting features relating to providing access to medical care or treatment</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_39" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.39</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if parents or carers fail to administer essential prescribed treatment for their child.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_40" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.40</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Suspect neglect if parents or carers fail to seek medical advice for their child to the extent that the child's health and wellbeing is compromised, including if the child is in ongoing pain.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_41" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.41</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if parents or carers repeatedly fail to bring their child to follow-up appointments that are essential for their child's health and wellbeing.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_42" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.42</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if parents or carers persistently fail to engage with relevant child health promotion programmes, which include:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>immunisation</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>health and development reviews</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>screening.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_43" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.43</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider neglect if parents or carers have access to but persistently fail to obtain treatment for their child's dental caries (tooth decay).</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_3_44" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.3.44</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Follow <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg89" target="_top" data-original-url="http://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/cg89">recommendations 1.2.11 and 1.2.12 in the NICE guideline on child maltreatment</a> if you have concerns about fabricated or induced illness. </p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Recognising child trafficking" id="ng76-recognising-child-trafficking">
      <h4 class="title" id="recognising-child-trafficking">Recognising child trafficking</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_45" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.45</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that children and young people may be trafficked for sexual exploitation and other reasons including:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>forced marriage</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>domestic servitude</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>working for low or no pay, or in illegal industries </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>being used for benefit fraud.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_46" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.46</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Recognise that both girls and boys can be trafficked and that children and young people can be trafficked within and from the UK, as well from other countries.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_3_47" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.3.47</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>If you suspect a child or young person may have been trafficked:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>make a referral to children's social care and the police</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>a designated first responder should make a referral to the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/human-trafficking-victims-referral-and-assessment-forms/guidance-on-the-national-referral-mechanism-for-potential-adult-victims-of-modern-slavery-england-and-wales" target="_top">government's National Referral Mechanism</a></p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>ensure that concerns about their age and immigration status do not override child protection considerations</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>recognise that choosing an interpreter from the child's community may represent to them the community that has exploited them</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>aim to ensure continuity with the same independent interpreter, keyworker or independent advocate.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.4 Assessing risk and need in relation to child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-assessing-risk-and-need-in-relation-to-child-abuse-and-neglect">
    <h3 class="title" id="assessing-risk-and-need-in-relation-to-child-abuse-and-neglect">1.4 Assessing risk and need in relation to child abuse and neglect</h3>
    <p>These recommendations are for practitioners undertaking:</p>
    <div class="itemizedlist">
      <ul class="itemizedlist">
        <li class="listitem">
          <p>
            <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#early-help" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#early-help">early help</a> assessment (undertaken by a <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#lead-professional" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#lead-professional">lead professional</a>) </p>
        </li>
        <li class="listitem">
          <p>assessment under Section 17 of the <a class="link" href="http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/17" target="_top">Children Act 1989</a> (led by a social worker) or </p>
        </li>
        <li class="listitem">
          <p>enquiry under Section 47 of the <a class="link" href="http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/section/47" target="_top">Children Act 1989</a> (led by a social worker, sometimes jointly with police). </p>
        </li>
      </ul>
    </div>
    <p>Refer to guidance on early help and statutory assessment in the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/working-together-to-safeguard-children--2" target="_top">Department for Education's statutory guidance on working together to safeguard children</a> as well as local protocols for assessment. The following recommendations highlight areas or practice which have been shown by evidence as being of particular importance, or as not always working well in practice.</p>
    <div class="section" title="Carrying out assessments" id="ng76-carrying-out-assessments">
      <h4 class="title" id="carrying-out-assessments">Carrying out assessments</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.1</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>During assessment: </p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>observe the child or young person, including their relationships with parents or carers</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>communicate directly with the child or young person without their parent or carer being present, with the parent or carer's consent </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>explore in a non-leading way any presenting signs or possible history of child abuse and neglect. <br /><br />Do not rely solely on information from the parent or carer in an assessment. See also <a class="link" href="recommendations#working-with-children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#working-with-children-and-young-people">recommendations 1.1.1 to 1.1.11</a> about working with children, young people, parents and carers.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.2</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>When assessing a child or young person follow the principles in <a class="link" href="recommendations#working-with-children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#working-with-children-and-young-people">recommendations 1.1.2 and 1.1.3</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.3</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>During assessment, focus primarily on the child or young person's needs but also remember to: </p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>address both the strengths and weaknesses of parents, carers and the wider family network </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>acknowledge that parenting can change over time, meaning that strengths and weaknesses are not fixed and should be reviewed</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>focus attention equally on male and female parents and carers.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.4</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>As part of assessment or enquiry into child abuse and neglect under the <a class="link" href="http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/contents" target="_top">Children Act 1989</a>, collect and analyse information about all significant people (including siblings) in the child or young person's care environment, unless it is not safe to do so (for example in cases of domestic abuse or <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#forced-marriage" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#forced-marriage">forced marriage</a>) or it could affect the nature of a criminal investigation. Use professional judgement to determine the risks and benefits of including people in assessment in these instances. Gather the following information about each person:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Their personal, social and health history.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Their family history, including experiences of being parented.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>Any adverse childhood experiences.</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>The quality of their relationship with the child or young person.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.5</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>As part of assessment or enquiry into child abuse and neglect under the <a class="link" href="http://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1989/41/contents" target="_top">Children Act 1989</a>, communicate your concerns honestly to families about child abuse and neglect. Take into account what information should be shared, and with whom, to avoid increasing the risk of harm to the child or young person (and adult victims in cases of domestic abuse).</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_6" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.6</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Organisations should ensure that practitioners conducting assessment in relation to abuse or neglect of disabled children or young people, or those with neurodevelopmental disorders, can access a specialist with knowledge about those children and young people's specific needs and impairments.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_7" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.7</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Analyse the information collected during assessment and use it to develop a plan describing what services and support will be provided. Make sure the plan is agreed with the child or young person and their family (also see <a class="link" href="recommendations#working-with-children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#working-with-children-and-young-people">recommendations 1.1.7</a> and <a class="link" href="recommendations#working-with-parents-and-carers" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#working-with-parents-and-carers">1.1.11</a>). <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#analysis" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#analysis">Analysis</a> should include evaluating the impact of any <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#vulnerability-factor" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#vulnerability-factor">vulnerability factors</a> and considering their implications for the child or young person.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_4_8" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.4.8</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Review assessments and plans regularly.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.5 Early help for families showing possible signs of child abuse or neglect" id="ng76-early-help-for-families-showing-possible-signs-of-child-abuse-or-neglect">
    <h3 class="title" id="early-help-for-families-showing-possible-signs-of-child-abuse-or-neglect">1.5 Early help for families showing possible signs of child abuse or neglect</h3>
    <p>These recommendations are for:</p>
    <div class="itemizedlist">
      <ul class="itemizedlist">
        <li class="listitem">
          <p>Practitioners involved in early help for families showing possible signs of child abuse and neglect. This could include those undertaking the lead professional role (including a GP, family support worker, teacher, health visitor or special educational needs coordinator) or those providing early help interventions, such as family support workers. </p>
        </li>
        <li class="listitem">
          <p>Commissioners of early help services for children, young people and families.</p>
        </li>
      </ul>
    </div>
    <p>These recommendations support the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/working-together-to-safeguard-children--2" target="_top">Department for Education's statutory guidance on working together to safeguard children</a> by highlighting evidence-based programmes that could be offered as part of early help support based on the evidence. </p>
    <div class="section" title="Supporting families at the early help stage" id="ng76-supporting-families-at-the-early-help-stage">
      <h4 class="title" id="supporting-families-at-the-early-help-stage">Supporting families at the early help stage</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.1</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Provide <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#early-help" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#early-help">early help</a> in line with local protocols and '<a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/working-together-to-safeguard-children--2" target="_top">Working together to safeguard children</a>', and based on an assessment of the needs of children, young people and families.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.2</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Discuss early help support and interventions with children, young people and families as part of building close working relationships with them and gaining their consent (see <a class="link" href="recommendations#working-with-children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#working-with-children-and-young-people">section 1.1</a> for principles for working with children, young people, parents and carers). Explain what the support will involve and how you think it may help. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.3</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Give children, young people and their families a choice of proposed interventions if possible. Recognise that some interventions may not suit that person or family.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.4</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Early help should include: </p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>practical support, for example help to attend appointments and details of other agencies that can provide food, clothes and toys</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>emotional support, including empathy and active listening, and help to develop strategies for coping.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.5</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Give families information about local services and resources, including advocacy, that they may find useful. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Knowledge and skills of practitioners who provide early help" id="ng76-knowledge-and-skills-of-practitioners-who-provide-early-help">
