      <h4 class="title" id="knowledge-and-skills-of-practitioners-who-provide-early-help">Knowledge and skills of practitioners who provide early help</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_6" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.6</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Commissioners and managers should ensure that all practitioners working at the early help stage:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>have an understanding of typical and atypical child development</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>are able to tailor interventions to the needs of the child or young person, parents and carers including any disability or learning disability</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>understand the parental vulnerability factors for child abuse and neglect (see <a class="link" href="recommendations#family-factors" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#family-factors">recommendations 1.2.3 to 1.2.5</a>)</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>are aware of the possibility of escalation of risk, particularly if family circumstances change</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>understand how to work with families as a whole in order to better support children and young people.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Parenting programmes" id="ng76-parenting-programmes">
      <h4 class="title" id="parenting-programmes">Parenting programmes</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_7" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.7</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider a parenting programme lasting at least 12 weeks for parents or carers at risk of abusing or neglecting their child or children. Tailor parenting programmes to the specific needs of parents or carers and children (see recommendations 1.5.9 to 1.5.12).</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_8" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.8</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>When selecting parenting programmes think about whether parents or carers would benefit from help to: </p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>develop skills in positive behaviour management</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>address negative beliefs about the child and their own parenting</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>manage difficult emotions, including anger.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_9" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.9</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider the <a class="link" href="http://www.triplep.net/glo-en/find-out-about-triple-p/" target="_top">Triple P – Positive Parenting Program</a> (attributional retraining and anger management) for mothers of young children (up to age 7), who are experiencing anger management difficulties.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_10" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.10</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider a parenting programme for vulnerable mothers (for example, those with a low level of education or income or aged under 18) of preschool children. It should be based on a planned activities training model and focus on equipping parents or carers to prevent challenging behaviour by:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>planning and explaining activities</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>establishing rules and consequences</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>ignoring minor misbehaviour and using positive interaction skills. <br /><br />This can be provided with or without support provided by text message between training sessions.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_11" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.11</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Consider the <a class="link" href="http://learning.nspcc.org.uk/services-children-families/parents-under-pressure" target="_top">Parents Under Pressure</a> programme for mothers taking part in methadone maintenance programmes. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_12" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.12</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For parents or carers who have substance misuse problems, include content in the parenting programme to help them address their substance misuse in the context of parenting. For example, help them to address parenting stress which may be a trigger for substance misuse.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Home visiting programmes" id="ng76-home-visiting-programmes">
      <h4 class="title" id="home-visiting-programmes">Home visiting programmes</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_13" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.13</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For parents or carers at risk of abusing or neglecting their child or children, consider a weekly home visiting programme lasting at least 6 months, for example the Healthy Families model. This should be in addition to universal health visiting services available through the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/healthy-child-programme-pregnancy-and-the-first-5-years-of-life" target="_top">Department of Health's healthy child programme</a>. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_14" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.14</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Identify parents and carers who could be supported by a home visiting programme during pregnancy or shortly after birth, wherever possible. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_15" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.15</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Ensure that the home visiting programme is agreed with families and includes:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>support to develop positive parent–child relationships, including:</p>
              <p>
                <div class="itemizedlist">
                  <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>helping parents to understand children's behaviour more positively</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>modelling positive parenting behaviours</p>
                    </li>
                    <li class="listitem">
                      <p>observing and giving feedback on parent–child interactions</p>
                    </li>
                  </ul>
                </div>
              </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>helping parents to develop problem-solving skills </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>support for parents to address the impact of any substance use, previous domestic abuse and mental health problems on their parenting</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>support to access other relevant services, including health and mental health services, substance misuse services, early years, educational services and other community services</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>referral to children's social care where necessary, for example if current domestic abuse is discovered. </p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_5_16" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.5.16</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Ensure that the programme of home visits is delivered by a practitioner who has been trained in delivering that particular home visiting programme.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.6 Multi-agency response to child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-multi-agency-response-to-child-abuse-and-neglect">
    <h3 class="title" id="multi-agency-response-to-child-abuse-and-neglect">1.6 Multi-agency response to child abuse and neglect</h3>
    <p>These recommendations are for practitioners working with children, young people and parents or carers where a child or young person has been abused or neglected, including those assessed as 'in need', likely to suffer significant harm or suffering significant harm. </p>
    <p>Practitioners must follow the 'Processes for managing individual cases' in the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/working-together-to-safeguard-children--2" target="_top">Department for Education's statutory guidance on working together to safeguard children</a>. These recommendations complement the statutory guidance by adding or emphasising detail which has been shown by evidence to be of particular importance, or not currently happening in practice. </p>
    <div class="section" title="Multi-agency working" id="ng76-multi-agency-working">
      <h4 class="title" id="multi-agency-working">Multi-agency working</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_6_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.6.1</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Practitioners supporting children and young people who have been assessed as being 'in need', or suffering (or likely to suffer) significant harm in relation to child abuse or neglect should:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>build relationships with other practitioners working with that family</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>organise handovers if new staff members from their agency become involved</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>ensure actions set out in the 'child in need' or child protection plan are completed.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Supporting children and young people" id="ng76-supporting-children-and-young-people">
      <h4 class="title" id="supporting-children-and-young-people">Supporting children and young people</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_6_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.6.2</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Practitioners supporting children and young people who have been assessed as being 'in need' or suffering (or likely to suffer) significant harm in relation to child abuse or neglect should, with leadership and coordination by the social worker, do the following as a minimum:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>protect them from further abuse or neglect</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>support them to explore aspects of their experience and express their feelings</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>provide early emotional support, including building emotional resilience and strategies for coping with symptoms such as nightmares, flashbacks and self-harm</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>assess their physical health needs</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>assess the need for further mental health support</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>support them to reduce the risk of future abuse if appropriate, for example if a young person is at risk of sexual exploitation.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Children and young people affected by domestic abuse" id="ng76-children-and-young-people-affected-by-domestic-abuse">
      <h4 class="title" id="children-and-young-people-affected-by-domestic-abuse">Children and young people affected by domestic abuse</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_6_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.6.3</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For guidance on domestic abuse, see <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph50/chapter/1-Recommendations#recommendation-10-identify-and-where-necessary-refer-children-and-young-people-affected-by" target="_top">recommendations 10 and 11 in the NICE guideline on domestic violence and abuse</a>. </p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Child trafficking" id="ng76-child-trafficking">
      <h4 class="title" id="child-trafficking">Child trafficking</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_6_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.6.4</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>When working with children and young people who have been trafficked, follow the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/safeguarding-children-who-may-have-been-trafficked-practice-guidance" target="_top">government's guidance on safeguarding children who may have been trafficked</a>.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.7 Therapeutic interventions for children, young people and families after child abuse and neglect" id="ng76-therapeutic-interventions-for-children-young-people-and-families-after-child-abuse-and-neglect">
    <h3 class="title" id="therapeutic-interventions-for-children-young-people-and-families-after-child-abuse-and-neglect">1.7 Therapeutic interventions for children, young people and families after child abuse and neglect</h3>
    <p>These recommendations are for:<ul class="itemizedlist"><li class="listitem"><p>Social workers and others coordinating support for children and young people, to help them decide what services to refer children and young people to.</p></li><li class="listitem"><p>Child and adolescent mental health practitioners (psychologists, psychotherapists, psychiatrists), practitioners in specialist family intervention teams (for example social workers) and voluntary sector agencies. </p></li><li class="listitem"><p>Strategic commissioners of services for children and young people who have been abused or neglected.</p></li></ul></p>
    <p>Where interventions are recommended for particular groups, this reflects the evidence base for this intervention. </p>
    <article id="ng76-1_7_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.7.1</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Discuss in detail with children, young people, parents and carers any interventions you offer them, explaining what the intervention will involve and how you think it may help (see <a class="link" href="recommendations#working-with-children-and-young-people" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="recommendations" data-original-url="#working-with-children-and-young-people">section 1.1</a> for principles for working with children, young people, parents and carers). </p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <article id="ng76-1_7_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.7.2</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Give children, young people, parents and carers a choice of proposed interventions if possible. Recognise that some interventions, although effective, may not suit that person or family.</p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <article id="ng76-1_7_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.7.3</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>The choice of intervention should be based on a detailed assessment of the child or young person.</p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <div class="section" title="Therapeutic interventions following child physical abuse, emotional abuse or neglect" id="ng76-therapeutic-interventions-following-child-physical-abuse-emotional-abuse-or-neglect">
      <h4 class="title" id="therapeutic-interventions-following-child-physical-abuse-emotional-abuse-or-neglect">Therapeutic interventions following child physical abuse, emotional abuse or neglect</h4>
      <p>This section provides a range of options for therapeutic interventions for children and young people who have experienced physical abuse, emotional abuse or neglect. Some interventions involve the parent or carer who abused or neglected the child, and others involve alternative carers such as foster carers or adoptive parents. An overview of interventions is shown below.</p>
      <p></p>
      <caption>
        <strong>Figure 1 Interventions following physical abuse, emotional abuse or neglect</strong>
      </caption>
      <div class="mediaobject" align="center">
        <div class="informaltable">
          <table border="0" summary="manufactured viewport for HTML img" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="601">
            <tr height="338">
              <td align="center">
                <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," align="middle" height="338" data-imagename="figure1.jpg" />
              </td>
            </tr>
          </table>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p>For more recommendations about looked-after children, including children in residential care, see the <a class="link" href="https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph28" target="_top" data-original-url="http://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ph28">NICE guideline on looked-after children and young people</a>.</p>
      <div class="section" title="Children under 5" id="ng76-children-under-5">
        <h5 class="title" id="children-under-5">Children under 5</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.4</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Offer an <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#attachment-based-intervention" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#attachment-based-intervention">attachment-based intervention</a>, for example Attachment and Biobehavioural Catch-up, to parents or carers who have neglected or physically abused a child under 5. </p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.5</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Deliver the attachment-based intervention in the parent or carer's home, if possible, and provide at least 10 sessions. Aim to:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>improve how they nurture their child, including when the child is distressed</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>improve their understanding of what their child's behaviour means</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>help them respond positively to cues and expressions of the child's feelings </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>improve how they manage their feelings when caring for their child.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_6" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.6</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider child–parent psychotherapy for parents or carers and their children under 5 if the parent or carer has physically or emotionally abused or neglected the child, or the child has been exposed to domestic violence.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_7" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.7</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Ensure that child–parent psychotherapy:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>is based on the Cicchetti and Toth model</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>consists of weekly sessions (lasting 45–60 minutes) over 1 year</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>is delivered in the parents' home, if possible, by a therapist trained in the intervention </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>involves directly observing the child and the parent–child interaction</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>explores the parents' understanding of the child's behaviour</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>explores the relationship between the emotional reactions of the parents and their perceptions of the child on the one hand, and the parents' own childhood experiences on the other hand.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_8" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.8</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Offer an attachment-based intervention in the home to <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#foster-carer" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#foster-carer">foster carers</a> looking after children under 5 who have been abused or neglected. Aim to help foster carers to:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>improve how they nurture their foster child, including when the child is distressed</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>improve their understanding of what the child's behaviour means</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>respond positively to cues and expressions of the child's feelings </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>behave in ways that are not frightening to the child </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>improve how they manage their feelings when caring for their child.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_9" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.9</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider the attachment-based intervention in recommendation 1.7.8 for adoptive parents and those providing permanence (including <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#special-guardian" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#special-guardian">special guardians</a>, foster carers or kinship carers) for children under 5 who have been abused or neglected.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Children aged 12 and under" id="ng76-children-aged-12-and-under">
        <h5 class="title" id="children-aged-12-and-under">Children aged 12 and under</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_10" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.10</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider a comprehensive <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#parenting-intervention" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#parenting-intervention">parenting intervention</a>, for example <a class="link" href="https://library.nspcc.org.uk/HeritageScripts/Hapi.dll/search2?searchTerm0=C5723" target="_top">SafeCare</a>, for parents and children under 12 if the parent or carer has physically or emotionally abused or neglected the child. This should be delivered by a professional trained in the intervention and comprise weekly home visits for at least 6 months that address: </p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>parent–child interactions </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>caregiving structures and parenting routines </p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>parental stress</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>home safety</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>any other issues that caused the family to come to the attention of services. <br /><br />As part of the intervention, help the family to access other services they might find useful.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_11" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.11</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider parent–child interaction therapy for parents or carers and children under 12 if the parent or carer has physically abused or neglected the child. Combine group sessions for these parents with individual child–parent sessions focusing on developing child-centred interaction and effective discipline skills.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_12" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.12</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Offer a group-based parent training intervention, for example KEEP, to foster carers of children aged 5 to 12 who have been abused or neglected and are showing problematic behaviours. Include strategies to manage behaviour and discipline positively. Provide group sessions over at least 16 weeks with groups of 8 to 10 foster carers, including video, role play and homework practice. </p>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_13" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.13</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider the intervention in recommendation 1.7.12 for foster carers of children aged 5 to 12 who have been abused or neglected and are not currently showing problematic behaviours. </p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
      <div class="section" title="Children and young people aged 17 and under" id="ng76-children-and-young-people-aged-17-and-under">
        <h5 class="title" id="children-and-young-people-aged-17-and-under">Children and young people aged 17 and under</h5>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_14" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.14</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider multi-systemic therapy for child abuse and neglect (MST‑CAN) for parents or carers of children and young people aged 10 to 17 if the parent or carer has abused or neglected their child. This should last 4 to 6 months and:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>involve the whole family</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>address multiple factors contributing to the problem</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>be delivered in the home or in another convenient location</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>include a round-the-clock on-call service to support families to manage crises.</p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_15" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.15</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>For foster carers of children and young people aged 5 to 17 who have been abused or neglected, consider a trauma-informed group parenting intervention, using a trust-based relational intervention as an example. It should last for at least 4 day‑long sessions and help foster carers to:</p>
            <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>develop the child or young person's capacity for self-regulation</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>build trusting relationships</p>
              </li>
              <li class="listitem">
                <p>develop proactive and reactive strategies for managing behaviour. </p>
              </li>
            </ul>
          </div>
        </article>
        <article id="ng76-1_7_16" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
          <h6 class="recommendation__number">1.7.16</h6>
          <div class="recommendation__body">
            <p>Consider the trauma-informed group parenting intervention in recommendation 1.7.15 for adoptive parents and those providing permanence (including special guardians, foster carers or kinship carers) for children aged 5 to 17 who have been abused or neglected.</p>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="section" title="Therapeutic interventions for children, young people and families after sexual abuse" id="ng76-therapeutic-interventions-for-children-young-people-and-families-after-sexual-abuse">
      <h4 class="title" id="therapeutic-interventions-for-children-young-people-and-families-after-sexual-abuse">Therapeutic interventions for children, young people and families after sexual abuse</h4>
      <p>This section provides a range of options for therapeutic interventions for children and young people who have experienced sexual abuse. An overview of interventions is shown below. </p>
      <caption>
        <strong>Figure 2. Interventions following sexual abuse</strong>
      </caption>
      <div class="mediaobject" align="center">
        <div class="informaltable">
          <table border="0" summary="manufactured viewport for HTML img" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="601">
            <tr height="142">
              <td align="center">
                <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," align="middle" height="142" data-imagename="figure2.jpg" />
              </td>
            </tr>
          </table>
        </div>
      </div>
      <article id="ng76-1_7_17" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.7.17</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Offer group or individual trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy over 12 to 16 sessions (more if needed) to children and young people (boys or girls) who have been sexually abused and show symptoms of anxiety, sexualised behaviour or post-traumatic stress disorder. When offering this therapy:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>discuss it fully with the child or young person before providing it and make clear that there are other options available if they would prefer </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>provide separate trauma-focused cognitive behavioural therapy sessions for the non-abusing parent or carer.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_7_18" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.7.18</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For children and young people (boys or girls) aged 8 to 17 who have been sexually abused, consider an intervention, for example 'Letting the future in', that:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>emphasises the importance of the therapeutic relationship between the child or young person and therapist </p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>offers support tailored to the child or young person's needs, drawing on a range of approaches including counselling, socio-educative and creative (such as drama or art)</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>includes individual work with the child or young person (up to 20 sessions, extending to 30 as needed) and parallel work with non-abusing parents or carers (up to 8 sessions).</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article id="ng76-1_7_19" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.7.19</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>For girls aged 6 to 14 who have been sexually abused and who are showing symptoms of emotional or behavioural disturbance, consider one of the following, after assessing carefully and discussing which option would suit her best:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>individual focused psychoanalytic therapy (up to 30 sessions) <strong>or</strong></p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>group psychotherapeutic and psychoeducational sessions (up to 18 sessions). <br /><br />Provide separate supportive sessions for the non-abusing parent or carer, helping them to support the child's attendance at therapy, as well as addressing issues within the family.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="section" title="1.8 Planning and delivering services" id="ng76-planning-and-delivering-services">
    <h3 class="title" id="planning-and-delivering-services">1.8 Planning and delivering services</h3>
    <p>These recommendations are for senior managers in agencies responsible for planning and delivering services to children and young people. They provide additional detail to the <a class="link" href="http://www.gov.uk/government/publications/working-together-to-safeguard-children--2" target="_top">Department for Education's statutory guidance on working together to safeguard children</a> on strategic arrangements for multi-agency working. </p>
    <article id="ng76-1_8_1" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.8.1</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Plan services in a way that enables children, young people, parents and carers to work with the same practitioners over time where possible.</p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <article id="ng76-1_8_2" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.8.2</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Agencies responsible for planning and delivering services for children and young people should agree terminology across agencies relating to child protection roles and processes, and ensure these are well publicised.</p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <article id="ng76-1_8_3" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.8.3</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>Ensure that local threshold documents set out responses to other forms of abuse including <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#child-sexual-exploitation" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#child-sexual-exploitation">child sexual exploitation</a>, <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#female-genital-mutilation" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#female-genital-mutilation">female genital mutilation</a>, <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#honour-based-abuse" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#honour-based-abuse">honour-based abuse</a> (including <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#forced-marriage" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#forced-marriage">forced marriage</a>), <a class="link" href="terms-used-in-this-guideline#child-trafficking-2" target="_top" rel="internal" data-chapter="terms-used-in-this-guideline" data-original-url="#child-trafficking-2">child trafficking</a>, serious youth violence and gang-related abuse. Ensure that these are communicated to local agencies, including those providing universal services, so that they are aware of these forms of abuse.</p>
      </div>
    </article>
    <article id="ng76-1_8_4" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
      <h4 class="recommendation__number">1.8.4</h4>
      <div class="recommendation__body">
        <p>To address the risks posed by sexual exploitation and gang-related abuse, agencies responsible for planning and delivering services for children and young people should put in place:</p>
        <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
          <li class="listitem">
            <p>effective leadership within agencies</p>
          </li>
          <li class="listitem">
            <p>a local lead who will coordinate planning and information sharing between agencies.</p>
          </li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </article>
    <div class="section" title="Supervision and support for staff" id="ng76-supervision-and-support-for-staff">
      <h4 class="title" id="supervision-and-support-for-staff">Supervision and support for staff</h4>
      <article id="ng76-1_8_5" class="numbered-paragraph recommendation">
        <h5 class="recommendation__number">1.8.5</h5>
        <div class="recommendation__body">
          <p>Organisations should support staff working with children and families at risk of or experiencing child abuse and neglect, and provide good quality supervision, tailored to their level of involvement in safeguarding work. This should include:</p>
          <ul class="itemizedlist indented">
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>case management</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>reflective practice</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>emotional support</p>
            </li>
            <li class="listitem">
              <p>continuing professional development.</p>
            </li>
          </ul>
        </div>
      </article>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>                        </div>




                    </div>

                </div>

                

            </div>

                <div data-g="12" class="hide-print">

                    </div>
                </div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>

</div>
